# dokploy_destination (Resource)

An S3-compatible storage destination used by `dokploy_backup`. Lives at the organization level.

## Example Usage

```terraform
resource "dokploy_destination" "s3" {
  name              = "prod-backups"
  provider_type     = "DigitalOcean"
  bucket            = "my-bucket"
  endpoint          = "https://sfo3.digitaloceanspaces.com"
  region            = ""
  access_key        = var.do_access_key
  secret_access_key = var.do_secret_key
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `access_key` (String) S3 access key id.
- `bucket` (String) S3 bucket name.
- `endpoint` (String) S3 endpoint URL.
- `name` (String) Display name.
- `provider_type` (String) S3 provider (free-form string; observed values: `DigitalOcean`, `AWS`). Changing this forces replacement.
- `secret_access_key` (String, Sensitive) S3 secret access key.

### Optional

- `additional_flags` (List of String) Extra flags forwarded to the backup tool (rclone-style).
- `region` (String) S3 region (empty for DO Spaces, required for AWS).

### Read-Only

- `id` (String) The ID of this resource.
- `organization_id` (String) Organization the destination belongs to (set by the API key).

## Import

Import is supported using the following syntax:

The [`terraform import` command](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/cli/commands/import) can be used, for example:

```shell
terraform import dokploy_destination.s3 <destinationId>
```
